phase_1.audio: Audio environment is distracting with avg 63.9 dB exceeding the 60 dB acceptable threshold. All 4 confidence-filtered events labeled 'busy' with 3 Speech and 1 Ambient AI labels indicate a noisy, conversation-heavy environment unsuitable for focused study.
phase_1.vitals: Heart rate at 86.9 bpm is slightly elevated above the calm focus range (60-85 bpm), suggesting mild stress or activity. Steps averaging 36.25 per sample and distance of 32.99m per sample indicate consistent movement rather than seated study behavior.
phase_1.gps_motion: GPS shows drastic location instability with 331.5m displacement and 838.6m total travel, far exceeding stable study thresholds (<200m). Motion context confirms active behavior with 3 walking instances vs only 1 stationary, indicating frequent movement throughout the session.

phase_2.correlations: All modalities converge on a pattern of active mobility rather than concentrated study: noisy busy audio aligns with walking motion contexts, elevated heart rate correlates with continuous movement (36 steps/sample), and substantial GPS travel (838m) confirms the user was mobile throughout. The combination of speech-heavy audio, consistent walking, and location changes suggests commuting, errands, or social activity rather than stationary study.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: This 77-minute session shows minimal to no study concentration. The user was physically active and mobile in a noisy environment with frequent speech, exhibiting none of the characteristics of focused study (quiet space, stationary position, stable location, calm physiology). This appears to be a non-study activity period such as traveling, socializing, or running errands.

phase_3.recommendations: 1) Establish a dedicated, stationary study location to eliminate the 838m of travel and walking interruptions observed in this session. 2) Choose a quiet environment with <60 dB ambient noise to avoid the distracting 'busy' and speech-heavy audio detected here. 3) Schedule study sessions separately from active/mobile periods; use movement-heavy times for breaks or errands rather than concentration work.

reason: The session exhibits severe concentration barriers across all modalities: distracting audio at 63.9 dB with busy/speech labels, elevated heart rate of 86.9 bpm with continuous movement (36 steps/sample), and drastic location instability (838m travel, 3 walking contexts). These indicators collectively demonstrate active mobility in a noisy environment rather than stationary, focused study behavior.
